sense.definition: relating to a condition where the body's immune system mistakenly attacks its own cells, tissues, or organs

examples.x: Type 1 diabetes results from an autoimmune attack on pancreas cells.
Researchers explore ways to control autoimmune responses.
In autoimmune conditions, the immune system can harm healthy tissues.

sense.definition: related to diseases that can be transmitted from one person to another through direct or indirect means

examples.x: COVID-19 is highly communicable, easily passing from person to person.
HIV is not easily communicable through casual contact.
Public health measures aim to contain communicable outbreaks.

sense.definition: (of an illness) difficult to cure and long-lasting

examples.x: Mary's chronic asthma requires daily medication to control her symptoms.
David's chronic depression affects his mood and energy levels on a daily basis.
Tom's chronic arthritis makes it challenging for him to move around comfortably.
Despite following a strict diet, Tim's chronic diabetes requires constant monitoring of his blood sugar levels.
Sarah's chronic migraine headaches often last for days, despite trying different medications.

sense.definition: (of disease or illness) likely to be transmitted from one person to another
(of an ilness) not fatal or harmful

examples.x: She received the news that her condition was benign, bringing relief to her and her family.
The doctor assured him that the lump on his skin was benign and required no treatment.
Despite experiencing symptoms, the tests revealed that his condition was benign and temporary.
The specialist explained that the irregular heartbeat was benign and manageable with medication.
The veterinarian informed the pet owner that the lump on their dog's paw was benign and did not require surgery.

sense.definition: having autism spectrum disorder, a developmental condition that affects social interaction, communication, and behavior

examples.x: The autistic child thrives in a structured environment with clear routines.
He has an autistic brother who communicates through nonverbal cues and gestures.
The autistic teenager has a talent for memorizing facts and details about their special interests.
She advocates for inclusive education to support autistic students in mainstream classrooms.
The autistic community advocates for acceptance, understanding, and inclusion.

sense.definition: (of a disease) not showing any symptoms associated with it

examples.x: Regular testing is essential to identify asymptomatic cases and prevent the spread of the virus.
The asymptomatic carrier unknowingly spread the disease to several people at the gathering.
Many individuals infected with the parasite are asymptomatic and may not seek medical attention.
The doctor explained that some people can be asymptomatic carriers of genetic disorders.
Despite being asymptomatic, the patient was advised to monitor their health closely for any signs of illness.

sense.definition: having a disease since birth that is not necessarily hereditary

examples.x: Mary's congenital condition caused her to be born with an extra finger on each hand.
Tim was born with a congenital hip dislocation that required corrective surgery.
Tom's congenital hearing loss was detected shortly after birth during a newborn screening.

sense.definition: (of a disease) transmittable from one person to another through close contact

examples.x: COVID-19 is highly contagious, spreading easily among individuals in crowded places.
The flu virus is contagious and can be transmitted through respiratory droplets.
Healthcare workers take precautions to prevent the spread of contagious illnesses in hospitals.
Quarantine measures were implemented to contain the outbreak of a contagious virus in the community.

sense.definition: characterized by the gradual deterioration or decline of a particular organ, system, or function in the body

examples.x: Degenerative disc disease is a condition that causes the breakdown of spinal discs, leading to pain and stiffness.
Aging is associated with degenerative changes in various bodily tissues.
Regular exercise can slow down the degenerative effects on the cardiovascular system.
Chronic exposure to certain substances may lead to degenerative organ damage.

sense.definition: having the symptoms of a fever, such as high temperature, sweating, shivering, etc.

examples.x: His febrile condition left him shivering under multiple blankets.
The patient’s febrile symptoms required immediate medical attention.
The doctor noted the febrile child’s flushed face and rapid breathing.
The febrile reaction indicated that he was battling a serious infection.
The febrile state was accompanied by chills and general weakness.

sense.definition: (of an illness) developing suddenly and progresses rapidly, often with severe and intense symptoms

examples.x: A fulminant infection can lead to rapid and severe deterioration of health.
The patient experienced a fulminant onset of symptoms, requiring emergency treatment.
Certain toxins can trigger fulminant poisoning with swift and severe consequences.
Fulminant heart failure can manifest as a sudden and acute cardiac crisis.

sense.definition: (of a disease or a sick person) impossible to cure or unresponsive to treatment
(of a disease or condition) capable of transmitting from one person, organism, or object to another through direct or indirect contact

examples.x: Sarah stayed home from work to avoid spreading her infectious cold to her coworkers.
Tom's infectious laughter spread throughout the room, causing everyone to smile.
The flu is highly infectious, spreading easily from person to person, especially in crowded places.
COVID-19 is an infectious respiratory illness caused by the coronavirus SARS-CoV-2, which has led to a global pandemic.

sense.definition: causing or involving swelling and irritation of body tissues

examples.x: Inflammatory skin disorders like psoriasis and eczema result in redness and itching of the skin.
The inflammatory response in the body was triggered by a bacterial infection, leading to a severe fever and swelling.
Inflammatory foods may exacerbate symptoms in individuals with certain autoimmune conditions.
Inflammatory responses play a crucial role in the body's defense against infections.

sense.definition: (of a tumor or disease) uncontrollable and likely to be fatal

examples.x: She underwent surgery to remove the malignant growth in her lung.
The oncologist recommended a combination of chemotherapy and radiation to combat the malignant disease.

sense.definition: regarding one's  mind, mental capacities, or aspects of mental well-being

examples.x: The chess game challenged him mentally, requiring strategic thinking and concentration.
The problem-solving activity engaged students mentally, fostering critical thinking.
The stress affected her mentally, influencing her ability to focus and make decisions.
The training program was designed to stimulate participants mentally, enhancing cognitive abilities.
The illness impacted him mentally, causing difficulties in memory and concentration.

sense.definition: having a gentle or not very strong effect

examples.x: Her mild response to criticism shows her maturity.
The medication has a mild effect and doesn't cause drowsiness.
She experienced mild discomfort after the procedure.
His mild reaction to the criticism was unexpected.
The earthquake was mild, causing no significant damage.

sense.definition: (of pathology) related to a diseased state or an abnormal condition, especially one that is severe or harmful

examples.x: The autopsy revealed morbid changes in the affected organs.
Researchers are studying the morbid mechanisms underlying the progression of the illness.
The doctor discussed the morbid aspects of the patient's medical condition.
A morbid pathology report indicated advanced stages of the disease.
Certain diagnostic tests help identify morbid changes in tissue structure.

sense.definition: relating to or caused by an illness or disease

examples.x: The doctor observed pathological changes in the patient's organs due to the progression of the disease.
Pathological examination of the tissue sample confirmed the presence of cancer cells.
The medical team conducted a pathological study of the patient's blood to diagnose the infection.
Pathological fractures occur as a result of weakened bones due to diseases like osteoporosis.
The pathological findings confirmed the presence of a rare genetic disorder.

sense.definition: (of a physical illness) caused or aggravated by mental factors, such as stress and anxiety
(of pathology) relating to a period when a disease is inactive, showing no apparent symptoms or progression

examples.x: Post-treatment, the disease became quiescent with no visible signs.
Some joint diseases can stay quiescent without obvious symptoms.
A quiescent state signifies a reduction in the disease's activity.
Certain diseases remain quiescent until activated, showing no symptoms.
Following successful treatment, the patient's symptoms remained quiescent.

sense.definition: related to conditions causing inflammation and pain in joints, muscles, or connective tissues

examples.x: Support groups provide coping strategies for rheumatic individuals
Proper diagnosis is crucial for effective treatment in rheumatic disorders.
Lifestyle changes, like regular exercise, alleviate symptoms in rheumatic patients.
Weather changes may worsen rheumatic symptoms, requiring precautions.
Medication and exercise help manage symptoms in rheumatic conditions.

sense.definition: (of an illness) having no cure and gradually leading to death

examples.x: Mary's grandmother was diagnosed with terminal lung cancer and was placed in hospice care.
Despite undergoing extensive treatment, the patient's cancer was terminal, and they were given a limited time to live.
Tim's father was in the final stages of his terminal illness, and the family gathered to say their goodbyes.
Sarah's aunt had a terminal illness that caused her health to deteriorate rapidly.
Emily's grandfather's terminal condition made it difficult for him to perform even simple daily tasks.

sense.definition: relating to or suffering from tuberculosis, a severe and contagious infection that mainly affects one's lungs
(of sickness or disease) tending to spread in a rapid manner

examples.x: An aggressive strain of the flu virus was causing widespread illness.
The aggressive nature of the infection required immediate treatment.
The patient was diagnosed with an aggressive form of leukemia.
Due to its aggressive spread, the disease was declared an epidemic.
The doctors were concerned about the aggressive cancer that had spread quickly.

sense.definition: caused by or relating to allergy

examples.x: The doctor prescribed antihistamines to relieve her allergic symptoms.
He had to avoid certain flowers because their pollen triggered his allergic asthma.
The child's skin became red and itchy due to an allergic response to a new soap.

sense.definition: relating to a health condition where a person has a lower than normal number of red blood cells, causing fatigue and weakness

examples.x: She felt constantly fatigued and weak due to being anemic, with her doctor recommending iron supplements to boost her red blood cell count.
Anemic individuals may experience symptoms such as dizziness, shortness of breath, and pale skin due to reduced oxygen-carrying capacity in the blood.
The doctor diagnosed her as anemic after conducting blood tests that revealed a low hemoglobin level.
Anemic patients often require regular monitoring of their blood counts and may need to make dietary changes or receive iron infusions to improve their condition.
Despite feeling tired all the time, she initially attributed her symptoms to stress until a blood test confirmed that she was anemic.

sense.definition: related to a condition or sound characterized by audible wheezing or whistling during breathing, typically caused by narrowed airways

examples.x: The child exhibited asthmatic breathing with a noticeable whistling sound.
Asthmatic wheezing can signal ongoing respiratory issues.
Certain triggers, like allergens, can induce asthmatic breathing in sensitive individuals.
Asthmatic inhalers are commonly prescribed to alleviate wheezing and improve breathing.
Asthmatic wheezing can be a symptom of an ongoing respiratory issue.

sense.definition: relating to a medical condition characterized by an impaired ability to regulate blood sugar levels

examples.x: Diabetic education programs provide valuable information on self-care practices and lifestyle adjustments.
The diabetic support group provided valuable resources and encouragement for managing the condition.
The research team focused on developing new technologies for diabetic care and monitoring.
The cookbook featured recipes tailored to diabetic dietary restrictions, emphasizing balanced and nutritious meals.

sense.definition: affected by a disease

examples.x: The diseased plants showed signs of wilting and discoloration due to a fungal infection.
The veterinarian examined the diseased dog and prescribed medication to treat its skin condition.
The doctor treated the patient's diseased liver with medication and recommended dietary changes to improve its function.
The scientist studied the diseased cells under a microscope to understand the mechanisms of the illness.
The diseased trees in the forest were marked for removal to prevent the spread of the invasive pest.

sense.definition: looking thin, pale, or exhausted due to prolonged period of suffering, anxiety, or starvation

examples.x: The refugees were found in an emaciated state after weeks without food or water.
The emaciated stray dog sought shelter under the abandoned building.
After months of chemotherapy, she emerged from treatment looking emaciated but hopeful.
The emaciated horse was rescued from neglect and given proper care.
The emaciated man's sunken eyes betrayed the depth of his suffering.

sense.definition: relating to an incurable chronic illness or a medical condition that will eventually lead to patient's death
related to or infected by malaria, a chronic disease that is caused by the bite of specific types of mosquito
inducing the urge to vomit

examples.x: The strong smell of rotten eggs is nauseous and can trigger vomiting.
Certain medications have a nauseous taste, making them difficult to swallow.
The sight of raw meat can be nauseous for some individuals.
The pungent odor of the chemicals was nauseous and unpleasant.
The nauseous fumes from the cleaning product filled the room.

sense.definition: caused by or related to a virus
(of a disease) able to make one sick
(of a disease or a crime) so chronic or serious that requires official notification and must be reported to the appropriate authorities
looking pale or sickly

examples.x: Despite her usual vibrant demeanor, Sarah looked peaky after staying up all night studying for exams.
Tim's friends grew concerned when they noticed him looking unusually peaky and fatigued.
Mary's sister seemed peaky after catching a cold, with dark circles under her eyes and a pale complexion.
Emily's coworker looked peaky after returning from a long business trip, suggesting she was exhausted.

sense.definition: affected by or belonging to celiac disease, a serious autoimmune disease and a digestive disorder that is triggered by eating foods that contain gluten
showing signs typical of a specific illness

examples.x: His rapid weight loss was symptomatic of an undiagnosed medical condition.
Her consistent fatigue was symptomatic of iron deficiency.

sense.definition: weak and thin, especially as a result of old age or an illness
not adequately fed or nourished therefore in bad health
lacking the necessary qualities, skills, or mental health to perform a task

examples.x: He was deemed unfit for the leadership role due to his lack of experience.
The candidate was unfit for the job because she didn't meet the required qualifications.
The doctor declared her unfit for work until she recovered.
He was unfit to participate in the marathon because of his recent illness.
The board concluded that he was unfit to manage the project due to his poor organizational skills.

sense.definition: not having a good physical or mental condition

examples.x: From his unhealthy appearance, it was clear that Tom had been skipping meals frequently.
Overuse of makeup made Susan look unhealthy and aged.
Prolonged stress can make you feel unhealthy and tired.
Sam looked unhealthy after several days of battling a flu.
With her pale complexion and low energy, Lisa seemed unhealthy to her friends.

sense.definition: lacking physical strength or energy

examples.x: After being sick for a week, he felt weak and tired.
Although he was physically weak, he had a sharp and agile mind.
The elderly man struggled to walk due to weak legs.
The patient's weak condition made it challenging for him to carry out daily activities.
The small dog may look weak, but it has a lot of energy and enthusiasm.

sense.definition: feeling or looking exhausted, unwell, or slightly ill, particularly after an intense physical activity
feeling sick or nauseous due to the motion of the ship or boat one is traveling with

examples.x: She felt seasick soon after the boat left the harbor.
The choppy waters made several passengers seasick during the cruise.
She had to lie down to relieve her seasick symptoms.
Despite the beautiful views, he felt too seasick to enjoy the boat ride.

sense.definition: not in a good and healthy physical or mental state

examples.x: I think the milk was bad; it made me sick.
My dog got sick and we visited the vet.
My father was so sick that he couldn't even get out of bed.
She stayed home because she is sick.
She was so sick, she missed the trip.

sense.definition: (of a headache) severe or massive
(of a disease or characteristic) able to be passed on to a child through the genes of its parents

examples.x: Hereditary traits, like eye color, are passed down from parents to their children.
They were aware of the hereditary risk of diabetes in their family.

sense.definition: (of diseases) passed on from one's parents
involving or suffering from anorexia
extremely emaciated, particularly due to illness, lack of food, or exposure to cold
an individual's ability to walk without stumbling and resist getting seasick while on a moving ship

examples.x: After a few days at sea, the crew members had finally gotten their sea legs and were able to move around the ship without stumbling.
The young boy was scared of the rocking boat at first, but his grandfather helped him find his sea legs and they spent the day fishing together.
